channel.title: DSP Guide – No Fluff, Just Fixes on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in DSP Guide – No Fluff, Just Fixes on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: 1 Artificial Sun Replaces 125 Wind Turbines - DSP Endgame Power Loop Guide
item.description:
Critical photons and antimatter are the gateway to endgame power in DSP. The loop is confusing at first: photons come from the sphere, make antimatter, make fuel rods, power your planet. Here is exactly how the chain works, step by step.

TL;DR: The Endgame Power Loop

Ray Receivers capture Dyson Sphere power and output Critical Photons. Particle Colliders turn photons into Antimatter + Hydrogen. Assemblers combine Antimatter with other materials to make Deuterium Fuel Rods or Antimatter Fuel Rods. Artificial Suns burn these rods for massive power. The loop produces more power than it consumes - but only if designed correctly.

item.title: Build a Mall in 30 Minutes - Never Hand-Craft a Building Again (DSP Guide)
item.description:
Hand-crafting buildings is the #1 time waste in DSP. You spend 20 minutes crafting assemblers, belts, and sorters when you should be designing production lines.

Here is how to build a mall - a factory that produces every building automatically, so you never craft by hand again.

TL;DR: The Mall Concept

A mall is a production line where raw materials go in one end and every building type comes out the other. Each building type (belts, sorters, assemblers, furnaces, etc.) has one dedicated assembler with its own storage box. You walk by and grab a full stack of whatever you need.

item.title: DSP Automation Guide (v0.10) - Build Your First Automated Factory in 10 Minutes
item.description:
Dyson Sphere Program does not hold your hand. Your first hour can feel overwhelming. Dozens of buildings, multiple resources, and no clear starting point.

Here is the exact 5-step path to get your first automated factory running.

Your First Factory: 5-Step Setup

Step 1: Place Your First Miner

Find iron ore on your starting planet. Open the build menu, select the Mining Machine. Place it directly on an iron ore patch. Connect it to power with a Wind Turbine.

item.title: DSP Blueprint Guide (v0.10) - Copy and Paste Entire Factories in Seconds
item.description:
DSP v0.10 added a proper blueprint system. No more hand-placing every smelter. Here is everything you need to know about blueprints - from basic copy-paste to advanced mirroring and sharing.

TL;DR: How Blueprints Work in DSP v0.10

Press B to open the Blueprint Library. Select buildings (hold shift to multi-select), press CTRL+C to copy, then CTRL+V to paste. That is the basic flow. This guide covers the details that are not in the game.

item.title: DSP Dyson Sphere Guide - Permanent First Layer Frame Strategy
item.description:
Building a Dyson Sphere is the endgame milestone in DSP. But the first attempt often fails: you launch sails, they expire, and you get zero power.

Here is the exact sequence to build a permanent, self-sustaining first layer.

TL;DR: The 4-Step Sequence

Build rockets first, not sails. A common trap: players launch thousands of solar sails and watch them expire (they last 1800 seconds - 30 minutes). Permanent sphere requires frame nodes, which require rockets. Build a rocket factory BEFORE scaling your sail launcher.

item.title: How to Fix DSP Yellow Matrix Research Slowdown After Tech Unlock
item.description:
You researched Titanium Alloy Smelting. Yellow matrices stopped. You researched Interstellar Logistics. Yellow matrices stopped again. Your research lab is consuming matrices but production is stalled. This isn't a bug — it's a buffer collapse, and it is fixable in 5 minutes.

TL;DR — The Fix:
Yellow matrix slowdown after tech unlock is caused by your production buffers running dry while new tech consumes the same materials. The fix is to add buffer storage between each production step and increase production of the bottleneck ingredient.
Quick check: Open your production stats (P key). Check which yellow matrix ingredient is empty. That's your bottleneck.

The Buffer Collapse: How It Works

Here is what actually happens when you research a big tech and your yellow science stops:

item.title: How to Set Up DSP Gravity Matrix Production With Mk.III Proliferator
item.description:
Purple science asks you to make Gravity Matrices — 20 processors, 20 quantum chips, and 2 plane filters per matrix. Without proliferator, you need dozens of buildings just to keep one matrix lab running. With Mk.III proliferator, the math changes completely.

TL;DR — The Setup:
Proliferate all three ingredients (Processors, Quantum Chips, Plane Filters) to Mk.III before inserting into the matrix lab. This gives you 4x production speed with no additional matrix labs.
The ratio: 1 Matrix Lab + 4 Proliferator Sprayers + dedicated production lines for each ingredient.

Why Mk.III Proliferator Changes Everything

Gravity Matrices require three complex ingredients: Processors (made from circuits + microcrystalline), Quantum Chips (from processors), and Plane Filters (from nanotubes). Each of these ingredients benefits from proliferator spray in two ways:

item.title: How to Prevent DSP Logistic Drone Congestion at ILS Station
item.description:
You have 200 drones orbiting a single ILS while three other stations sit empty. Items that should take 10 seconds to move take 5 minutes. Your factory is running at 30% because of logistics gridlock. Drone congestion is the hidden throughput killer of DSP.

TL;DR — The Fix:
Cap your drones per ILS station (5-10 max), use priority splitters to balance material distribution, and add orbital buffer chests near high-traffic buildings.
The magic number: 8 drones per ILS station is the sweet spot for 95% of factory setups.

What Causes Drone Gridlock

The drone congestion problem is a classic tragedy of the commons in DSP's logistics system. Here is the chain reaction:

item.title: How to Fix ILS Not Picking Up Items From DSP Local Storage
item.description:
You have 2,000 iron ingots sitting inside the ILS storage. The drones are idle. Nothing is moving. Your factory downstream is starving. This is the most common ILS troubleshooting issue in DSP.

TL;DR — The Fix:
Open the ILS panel. Set the slot to Supply (not Storage). Remote Demand set to 0. Local Demand set to 0 or your target. Drones will now pick up from this station.
The golden rule: "Supply" means drones can TAKE items out. "Storage" means items just sit there.

The #1 Culprit: Slot Mode Mismatch

The ILS has three slot states per item type: Supply, Demand, and Storage. These are NOT the same as the station-level "Local Supply / Local Demand / Remote Supply / Remote Demand" settings. They are per-slot behaviors that many players confuse.

item.title: How to Clear Hydrogen Backup in DSP Without Stopping Power Grid
item.description:
Your thermal plants flicker. Refineries stall. Power drops to 20%. You check the grid and find hydrogen backed up all the way to your refineries. Every DSP player hits this wall around Yellow Science.

TL;DR — The Fix:
Build a thermal power plant, connect it to your hydrogen line, and split off your excess hydrogen to burn for power.
Key insight: You do NOT need to stop your factory. You need to consume the excess hydrogen before it blocks your refineries. One thermal plant burning hydrogen at 100% upkeep can sink your entire hydrogen overflow.

The Core Problem: Why Hydrogen Traps Your Grid

Hydrogen backup is a design paradox: your refineries need to output hydrogen to keep running, but when your hydrogen buffers are full, the refineries stall. When refineries stall, you stop producing oil products (refined oil, graphite, and the red science flowing through them). Your entire early-to-mid game chain collapses.

item.title: DSP Energy Matrix Guide (v0.10) - Fuel Your Dyson Sphere Without Hydrogen Bottlenecks
item.description:
Energy Matrix is the energy currency that powers Dyson Sphere construction. Every Solar Sail launched, every Ray Receiver powered, every component of the Dyson Sphere itself — all of it requires Energy Matrix. It's the fuel of the endgame, and getting a reliable Energy Matrix supply running is one of the most satisfying milestones in DSP.

This guide covers the complete Energy Matrix production chain, the recipes that matter, how to scale it, and how proliferation affects your output.

item.title: DSP Logistics Guide (v0.10) - Drones vs Belts: When Each System Saves Your Factory
item.description:
DSP gives you two ways to move materials: belts and drones. Belts are local, simple, and fast. Drones are flexible, cross-planetary, and require zero physical infrastructure. Knowing which to use — and when — is one of the core strategic decisions in DSP factory design.

This guide breaks down throughput, use cases, cost, and scalability for each system.

TL;DR — Use belts for high-throughput local transport (within a planet). Use logistics drones for cross-planetary transport and flexible low-volume requests. Use vessels for massive interplanetary shipments of raw materials. For maximum efficiency, combine all three — belts for planet-level production, drones and vessels for network-level logistics.

Belts Win Where Throughput Matters - Local Transport That Never Drops a Packet

Belt transport moves materials along physical paths. They're fast, reliable, and perfect for any situation where you're moving high volumes of materials between buildings on the same planet.

item.title: DSP Mining Outpost Guide (v0.10) - Set Up Off-World Bases That Run Forever
item.description:
Every DSP factory runs on raw materials — and raw materials live on planets that are rarely the planet where your main factory sits. Mining outposts are how you get coal from a gas giant's moon, titanium from an ice world, and fire ice from the dark reaches of the galaxy without leaving your main factory's logistics grid.

This guide covers how to build mining outposts, position mining machines correctly, and connect them to your logistics network so resources flow automatically.

item.title: DSP Research Lab Guide (v0.10) - Automate Red to Purple Science Without Hand-Feeding
item.description:
Research is the clock in DSP. Every unlock — new buildings, new recipes, new logistics options — lives behind the research tree. And research lives behind science packs. If your science pack production is weak, your entire progression grinds to a halt.

This guide covers the complete science pack chain from Red Science to Purple Science, lab setup ratios, power requirements, and which research priorities actually matter for your factory's next phase.

item.title: DSP Smelting Array Blueprint Guide (v0.10) - 3 Smelters Feed a Mk.III Belt Perfectly
item.description:
Smelting is where every DSP run starts, and where most players first feel the pain of scaling up. One smelter looks fine. Ten smelters look decent. Fifty smelters feeding a sprawling factory reveal every bottleneck you buried under "it should work."

This guide covers smelting from the first furnace on a new planet through to full production columns — the correct ratios, the belt speeds that matter, and the layouts that scale cleanly without requiring you to redesign everything at every tier.

item.title: Blue Science Stuck? Fix Refinery Stalls and Hydrogen Backups (DSP Guide)
item.description:
You have got Red Science humming. Yellow Matrix is on the horizon. Then you build your first Refinery and everything stops.

Blue Science is the first wall in DSP that is not just "build more miners." It introduces Refined Oil, Hydrogen byproducts, and the frustrating "one output backed up, whole chain dead" problem. This guide walks through the exact setup so your Blue Matrix runs without constant babysitting.

TL;DR Three things to get right:
1. Hydrogen is a byproduct, not waste; give it somewhere to go or refineries stall
2. X-Ray Cracking is your release valve when Refined Oil backs up
3. Titanium Ingots are required for Blue Matrix; set that up first

Why Blue Science Is Different

Every science tier before Blue is straightforward. Magnetic coils go into Red. Gears and circuits, fine. Then Blue arrives and the game hands you a Refinery that takes one input and produces two outputs. Two outputs means two belts. If either belt fills up, the machine stops.

item.title: DSP Fire Ice & Deuterium: Cold Planet Refining Guide
item.description:
You've found a lava world or an ice giant. The stars call it a Fire Ice planet — and it's full of something that doesn't exist on temperate worlds: Deuterium. Deuterium is your path to advanced fuels, high-output Rocket Fuel, and the Yellow Science chain.

But getting it out of the ground and into your factory requires understanding a completely different logistics problem.

This guide covers finding Fire Ice planets, the extraction and refining mechanics, managing the water byproduct, and how to integrate Deuterium into your endgame production.

item.title: DSP Oil & Plastics: Full Refining Chain Guide
item.description:
Somewhere between your first smelter array and your first interplanetary logistics network, every DSP player hits the same wall: oil. Crude oil is everywhere once you find a sea on another planet, but turning it into something useful — Plastics, Rocket Fuel, or even just keeping your refineries from backing up — is a system that catches almost everyone off guard.

This guide covers the complete Oil Refining chain from extraction to end products, with working production ratios, the critical hydrogen byproduct problem, and how to automate it without tearing your hair out.

item.title: DSP Quantum Chips & Proliferator: Advanced Production Guide
item.description:
Every factory has a ceiling. At some point you're running 40 assemblers on your Circuit Board chain and wondering if there's a better way. In DSP v0.10, there is: Proliferator.

It's a resource you produce, spray onto your production lines, and watch your output climb without adding a single new assembler.

This guide covers the Quantum Chip production chain and explains the Proliferator system — what it is, how to produce it, what it does, and how to use it without turning your factory into a confusing mess of spray towers.

item.title: DSP Solar Sails & Ray Receivers: Complete Endgame Power Guide
item.description:
You've got a handful of coal power plants. Maybe you've built a wind farm or twelve. You're researching toward the stars — literally — and then it hits you: the Dyson sphere isn't just the goal, it's the power solution. To get there, you need Solar Sails and Ray Receivers working in tandem.

One launches into orbit and reflects stellar radiation. The other catches that energy and converts it into usable power for your planet.

item.title: Can't Find Titanium in DSP? Here's How to Get It (And Bring It Home)
item.description:
Your starter planet has no titanium.

That's not a bug. It's not bad luck. It's literally how the game works. The devs designed it this way to force you to figure out interplanetary travel.

Here's the quickest way to get titanium and why you shouldn't panic.

TL;DR: Fly to the planet next door. Mine the shiny blue rocks. Fly back with a full inventory. Build your first ILS to automate it. Done.

Step 1: Find Your Titanium Planet

Open your star map (M key). Look at the other planets in your starting system. One of them will have titanium veins. In the default cluster, your starter planet orbits the sun with two more planets — the one closer to the sun usually has titanium. Sometimes both do.

item.title: Green Science and Warpers: Stop Being Scared of Green Matrix (DSP Guide)
item.description:
You've got Yellow Science running. Titanium flowing. Everything is… fine. Then you look at Green Matrix. Three ingredients. Processors. Casimir Crystals. Electromagnetic Turbines.

And you think: "Yeah no. I'll do it later."

I get it. Every DSP player hits this wall. But here's the thing — Green Matrix unlocks Space Warpers. And Warpers turn your factory from a solar-system-only operation into a galactic empire. You want that.

TL;DR — Green Matrix needs 3 things: Processors + Casimir Crystals + EM Turbines. Scale your existing Processor line. Build a Casimir plant. That's most of the work done.

The Three Things You Need

Green Matrix = Processors + Casimir Crystals + EM Turbines

item.title: ILS Not Moving Items? Here's the 30-Second Fix (DSP Guide)
item.description:
Your ILS has power. Ships are docked. Nothing moves.

Yeah. This is the #1 “why the hell isn't this working” question in DSP. Let's fix it in 30 seconds flat.

TL;DR — Two things to check:
1. One planet must be Supply, the other Demand (not both Supply)
2. Your vessels need Space Warpers to fly. Put some in the slot.

Supply vs Demand — The 1-Minute Show

Every ILS slot has a dropdown: Supply, Demand, and Store. Half the community doesn't know these make the difference between a dead ILS and a humming factory.

item.title: Why Your DSP Power Grid Keeps Crashing (And How to Fix It)
item.description:
Everything is running. Then: darkness. Sorters moving like they're underwater. Your thermal plants flickering on and off. The death spiral.

Here's what happened and how to fix it.

TL;DR — The cascade:
Factory drains power → sorters slow down → thermal plants don't get fuel → production drops → more power loss → everything dies.
The fix: Disconnect non-essential buildings. Feed thermal plants manually. Then build more solar.

⚡ What Just Happened (The Cascade)

DSP has a nasty feedback loop that first-timers always run into:

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: May 19, 2026

This Privacy Policy describes how DSP Guide ("we", "our", or "the Site") collects, uses, and shares information when you visit our website at dsp-guide.com.

Information We Collect

Google Analytics

We use Google Analytics (GA4), a web analytics service provided by Google LLC. Google Analytics uses cookies and similar technologies to collect and analyze information about how visitors use the Site. This information may include:
